Name: Weedle
Effects: Put 1 random Weedle from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text:
Often found in forests and grasslands.
It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

Name: Beedrill ex
Effects: Discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Pinsir
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers.
Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

Name: Sprigatito
Effects: Put 1 random [G] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text:
Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants.
This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.

Name: Floragato
Flavor-Text: Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.

Name: Meowscarada
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

Name: Charmander
Flavor-Text:
It has a preference for hot things.
When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

Name: Charmeleon
Flavor-Text:
It has a barbaric nature.
In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws.

Name: Charizard ex
Effects: Take 3 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text:
Magmar dispatches its prey with fire.
But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Magmortar
Effects:
During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin.
If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
When heated by fire energy, its horns can get hotter than 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.
Those gored by them will suffer both wounds and burns.

Name: Tentacool
Flavor-Text:
Tentacool is not a particularly strong swimmer.
It drifts across the surface of shallow seas as it searches for prey.

Name: Tentacruel
Flavor-Text:
When the red orbs on Tentacruel’s head glow brightly, watch out.
The Pokémon is about to fire off a burst of ultrasonic waves.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back.
It deflates the sac before it dives.

Name: Wiglett
Effects:
1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random.
Do 30 damage to it.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Wugtrio ex
Effects:
1 of your opponent's Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times.
For each time a Pokémon was chosen, do 50 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food.
It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

Name: Tatsugiri
Ability-Effect: Your Active Dondozo has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text:
This is a small dragon Pokémon.
It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

Name: Pikachu ex
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text:
It rolls to move.
If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets.
But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It’s one of the kinds of Pokémon with electric cheek pouches.
It shoots charges from its tail.

Name: Pawmi
Flavor-Text:
It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks.
These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.

Name: Pawmo
Flavor-Text: When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.

Name: Pawmot
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.

Name: Abra
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon uses its psychic powers while it sleeps.
The contents of Abra’s dreams affect the powers that the Pokémon wields.

Name: Kadabra
Flavor-Text:
Using its psychic power, Kadabra levitates as it sleeps.
It uses its springy tail as a pillow.

Name: Alakazam
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any Energy attached.
Flavor-Text:
It has an incredibly high level of intelligence.
Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects:
Flip 4 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.

Name: Drifloon
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Drifblim
Flavor-Text:
It can generate and release gas within its body.
That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

Name: Giratina ex
Ability-Effect:
Once during your turn, you may take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Gimmighoul
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago.
It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text:
Its whole body is composed of muscles.
Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Hitmonlee
Flavor-Text:
This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance.
It can kick in succession from any position.

Name: Hitmonchan
Flavor-Text:
Its punches slice the air.
They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text:
They communicate with one another using their auras.
They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Lucario ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Flamigo
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

Name: Ekans
Flavor-Text:
By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself.
After a meal, it curls up and rests.

Name: Arbok
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: After stunning its opponents with the pattern on its stomach, it quickly wraps them up in its body and waits for them to stop moving.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land.
The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Paldean Clodsire ex
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Shroodle
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Grafaiai
Ability-Effect:
Once during your turn, you may flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats.
Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

Name: Tinkatink
Flavor-Text: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

Name: Tinkatuff
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

Name: Tinkaton ex
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Varoom
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Revavroom
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks.
It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Gholdengo
Effects:
Flip a coin for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins.
This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

Name: Rattata
Flavor-Text:
Its incisors grow continuously throughout its life.
If its incisors get too long, this Pokémon becomes unable to eat, and it starves to death.

Name: Raticate
Flavor-Text: People say that it fled from its enemies by using its small webbed hind feet to swim from island to island in Alola.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text:
It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody.
If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

Name: Wigglytuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It has a very fine fur.
Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

Name: Lickitung
Flavor-Text:
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in.
The itch won’t go away, either.

Name: Lickilicky
Flavor-Text:
Lickilicky’s strange tongue can stretch to many times the length of its body.
No one has figured out how Lickilicky’s tongue can stretch so far.

Name: Bidoof
Flavor-Text:
With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it.
It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bibarel ex
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Buneary
Flavor-Text:
If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind.
It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

Name: Lopunny
Flavor-Text:
Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings.
If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overacceleration attack does +20 damage.
Flavor-Text:
Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times.
Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

Name: Iono
Description: Each player shuffles the cards in their hand into their deck, then draws that many cards.

Name: Pokemon Center Lady
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

Name: Red
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex.

Name: Team Rocket Grunt
Description: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Meowscarada
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It inflates its flotation sac, keeping its face above water in order to watch for prey movement.

Flavor-Text:

It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Poke Ball
Description: Put 1 random Basic Pokemon from your deck into your hand.
